method: Ledger entry-word vs non-entry-word structural contrast (L2/L3). Two functional word classes defined by OBSERVABLE structure only: ENTRY-word = 'word' token whose immediately-following stream token is 'num'; NON-ENTRY word = 'word' token whose following token is not 'num' (or last). Metrics: (a) mean word LENGTH (n signs); (b) A-INITIAL rate (fraction of >=1-sign words whose first sign is 'A'). Contrast = entry minus non-entry. NULL = label permutation: permute entry/non-entry labels across the SAME word-token set (preserving class counts), recompute contrast, >=2000 draws, two-sided p. LB positive control gates the verdict (detect planted length gap + false-positive rate <=0.10).
key_findings: PRIMARY metric (word LENGTH): global entry-vs-nonentry contrast is -0.059 signs (entry words marginally SHORTER: 1.801 vs 1.860) and NOT significant under label permutation (two-sided p=0.211, >0.05). The frozen rule therefore yields ENTRY_CLASS_NO_CONTRAST.

POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED: the label-permutation machinery DETECTS a planted LB length gap (p=0.0005, correct positive direction, contrast=+2.34) and has a FALSE-POSITIVE rate of 0.0 (<=0.10) over 30 same-distribution splits. The null verdict is not a machinery artifact.

SECONDARY metric (A-INITIAL rate): global contrast IS significant (entry less A-initial: 0.0298 vs 0.0707, p=0.001). But A-rate is secondary/reported only and does not override the primary length verdict.

DIRECTION IS HETEROGENEOUS ACROSS SITES: of 4 sites individually significant for length, Haghia Triada shows entry words LONGER (+0.31) while Khania, Tylissos, Malia show entry words SHORTER (-0.44, -0.92, -1.08). Direction is NOT consistent (the global null is the honest summary).

LEAVE-ONE-SITE-OUT reveals Haghia Triada masks an opposite effect: excluding it, the global length contrast becomes -0.561 (p=0.0005) — i.e. outside Haghia Triada entry words are clearly shorter, but this is site-local heterogeneity, not a robust corpus-wide structural role contrast.

8 sites are testable (>=15 words in EACH class), so the verdict is NOT underpowered; the negative result reflects a genuine absence of a consistent corpus-wide length contrast between the two functional classes.
successor_hypotheses: E034: Decompose the Haghia Triada length signal by SUPPORT/CONTEXT (tablet vs other) — does the entry-longer effect concentrate in a specific document class, suggesting a local scribal convention rather than a structural role?
E035: Test whether the A-INITIAL contrast (significant globally, entry less A-initial) is itself robust across sites — it may mark a real structural role difference that LENGTH does not capture.
E036: Refine the functional classes by POSITIONAL structure (e.g. first-word-of-line entry vs mid-line entry; non-entry words adjacent to 'div' vs 'nl') to see if finer structural roles separate.
E037: Cross-check the entry/non-entry contrast against E031's ledger-grammar regularity per site — do sites with stricter WORD-NUMERAL grammar show a different structural profile?

E038: Test sign-repetition / sign-diversity (structural complexity) rather than raw length as the role-discriminating statistic — entry words may differ in internal sign structure even if length is matched.

E039: Examine whether the entry-class is dominated by a small set of recurrent word-forms (E032 recurrence) that happen to be short, which would explain the negative length contrast outside Haghia Triada.
non_circular: Tokens carry NO phonetic/sound/meaning/reading. Only L2/L3 structural statistics (word length in signs; A-initial indicator) computed on anonymous sign sequences. The entry/non-entry split is defined by OBSERVABLE stream structure (adjacency to a 'num' token), not by any reading. No numeral-value arithmetic is used. LB is a positive-control benchmark ONLY (planted length gap + same-distribution false-positive calibration); it does not inform the LA verdict. The label-permutation null is calibrated by construction (exchangeable under H0).
